weapon.apply-damage-disabled: Only a Keeper, or a user who can update both the target and this chat card, may apply damage.
weapon.damage-applied: {userName} applied {value} damage to {targetName}. Guts is now {guts}.
weapon.modifier-close-heavy: Heavy ranged weapons cannot be used in close combat.
weapon.modifier-close-light: Close combat with light ranged weapon: no penalty
weapon.modifier-close-modest: Close combat with modest ranged weapon: +1 Drag
weapon.modifier-no-source: No source token available; range modifiers were not applied.
weapon.modifier-no-target: No target selected; range modifiers were not applied.
weapon.modifier-out-of-range: Target is beyond this weapon's maximum range.
weapon.no-damage-penetrates: {targetName}'s armour on the {locationName} absorbs the blow.
weapon.no-target: No target selected. Attack rolled without hit location or damage application.
weapon.no-target-range: No target selected; range modifiers were not applied. Attack rolled without hit location or damage application.

abbrAccess.name: Abbreviate Access Names
abbrAccess.hint: Shortens the Access level names the way the book does. (e.g. "Common" becomes "C")

condensedRange.name: Condense Weapon Range Input
condensedRange.hint: With this enabled, the weapon range will be displayed as "X / Y" when editing a weapon. While disabled it will be as displayed as two different rows, one for Short Range and one for Long Range

sandsOfFateInitial.name: Sands of Fate Initial
sandsOfFateInitial.hint: What value should The Hourglass reset to when a Cryptic Event occurs

onCrypticEvent.name: Cryptic Event Alert
onCrypticEvent.hint: What happens when a cryptic event occurs by clicking the "Next Delve Tour" button in the HUD

allowUpdateSandsSocket.name: Player Haste Updates the Sands of Fate
allowUpdateSandsSocket.hint: This setting determines if when a player makes a haste check that the result will automatically be applied to the global Sands of Fate. Disabling this is good if you want to let players roll without needing to worry about automation messing anything up while they spam rolls.

a11y.guts-value-edit: The current amount of guts the character has
a11y.guts-value-readonly: The current amount of guts the character has
a11y.guts-max-readonly: The maximum amount of guts the character can have

Apps.heroic-hit-location-prompt: {actorName} scored a Heroic Success against {targetName} with {weaponName}. Choose the hit location.
Apps.RichEditor-no-collaborative: Warning: This editor is not collaborative, that means that if you and someone else are editing it at the same time, you won't see that someone else is making changes until they save, and then your changes will be lost.

CombatTracker.friendly-first-label: Heroes go first, click to make geists go first
CombatTracker.hostile-first-label: Geists go first, click to make heroes go first

error.cannot-equip: Cannot equip the {itemType}, see console for more details.
error.invalid-delta: The delta for "{name}" is not a number, cannot finish processing the action.
error.at-favourite-limit: Cannot favourite more than three items, unfavourite one to make space.
error.invalid-socket: Invalid socket data received, this means a module or system bug is present.
error.unknown-socket-event: An unknown socket event was received: {event}
error.no-active-gm: No active @USER.GM is logged in, you must wait for a @USER.GM to be active before you can do that.
error.malformed-socket-payload: Socket event "{event}" received with malformed payload. Details: {details}

warn.cannot-go-negative: "{name}" is unable to be a negative number.
warn.fazed-no-actions: Fazed: this subject may not take actions.
warn.fearful-restricted-actions: Fearful: this subject may only break, move, or run away from hostile combatants.
warn.unsupported-drop: This actor sheet only supports dropping Items.
warn.unsupported-drop-document: Dropping {documentName} documents onto this actor sheet is not supported.
warn.unsupported-item-drop: Cannot drop the item "{itemName}" of type "{itemType}" onto this actor sheet.
warn.weapon-attack-cannot-apply-damage: You do not have permission to apply damage to that target or update this chat card.
warn.weapon-attack-damage-already-applied: Damage from this attack has already been applied.
warn.weapon-attack-missing-actor: Unable to start a weapon attack without an actor.
warn.weapon-attack-missing-weapon: Unable to start a weapon attack without a weapon.
warn.weapon-attack-multiple-targets: Multiple targets selected. Using the first targeted token.
warn.weapon-attack-invalid-target: The selected target does not have a valid actor to damage.
warn.weapon-attack-invalid-attribute: {weaponName} does not have a valid attack attribute configured.
warn.weapon-attack-out-of-range: Target is beyond this weapon's maximum range.
warn.weapon-attack-heavy-close-combat: Heavy ranged weapons cannot be used in close combat.
warn.weapon-attack-range-no-target: No target selected; range modifiers were not applied.
warn.weapon-attack-range-no-source: No source token available; range modifiers were not applied.
warn.weapon-attack-target-missing: The targeted token or actor is no longer available.

auras.normal: The distance of your aura normally
auras.heavy: The distance of your aura when using Heavycraft

able.name: Able
able.description: Able weapons can be used two-handed or one-handed with a shield. When used with two hands, able weapons are considered long.

agile.name: Agile
agile.description: Attacks with Agile weapons may be made with Gait instead of Grit.

ammo.name: Ammo
ammo.description: Weapons with this trait require ammunition for use.

crude.name: Crude
crude.description: Crude or improvised weapons aren’t designed for combat. They’re ineffective against armor, unreliable, break with wear, and do 1 damage.

heavy.name: Heavy
heavy.description: Heavy weapons require two hands and cannot be equipped with other weapons or a shield. Their size, weight, and durability maximizes damage. Attacks made after moving with heavy weapons drag. Heavy ranged weapons may not be used in close combat.

light.name: Light
light.description: Light weapons can be used with one hand. Their light build makes them susceptible to wear. Light ranged weapons can be used without drag in close combat.

long.name: Long
long.description: Long weapons extend reach to diagonal spaces so that attacks can be made diagonally without drag.

loud.name: Loud
loud.description: Targets damaged by loud weapons take 1 morale effect.

modest.name: Modest
modest.description: Modest weapons are designed to maximize efficiency and effectiveness with one hand. They balance weight, durability, and capability. Modest ranged weapons drag in close combat.

reload.name: Reload
reload.description: Reload weapons are assumed loaded to start but must be reloaded once a shot has been fired. All Reload weapons also have the ammo trait.

thrown.name: Thrown
thrown.description: Thrown weapons are light hand weapons that can optionally be thrown to make short ranged attacks.

corrosive.name: Corrosive
corrosive.description: Corrosive creatures secrete an acidic mucus that damages metal. These creatures cause one point of wear on metal weapons when attacks hit.

entangle.name: Entangle
entangle.description: Some creatures have whip-like tentacles used to bind threats to protect themselves. If combatants are hit by creatures with this trait, they must rival for Gait. If they fail, they are entangled and are not able to move or attack until they successfully break as their next action.

fear.name: Fear
fear.description: Some creatures are so terrifying that even moving towards them demands incredible resolve. To move towards a creature with this trait, heroes must successfully rival for Grip. Failure makes the hero Fearful until a morale trial is passed.

flying.name: Flying
flying.description: Flying creatures follow the rules for flying movement in combat.

immunity.name: Immunity
immunity.description: Creatures with immunity have either an impenetrable aura or none at all and cannot be targeted with craft.

large.name: Large
large.description: Large creatures are hard to miss. Any attempt to attack or shoot a creature with this trait has edge.

poison.name: Poison
poison.description: Many species produce lethal enzymes. If combatants are damaged, they must rival for Grit. If they fail, they are poisoned. Poisoned combatants take 1 damage each morale phase until a successful recovery is made.

regeneration.name: Regeneration
regeneration.description: Some species have a super fast metabolism and heal at an unnatural rate. Subjects with this trait recover 1 damage each morale phase in combat.

smog.name: Smog
smog.description: Some creatures exude toxic gas. To move within reach, successfully rival for Grip. If heroes fail, they are poisoned.

stealth.name: Stealth
stealth.description: Creatures with stealth are fast and agile, making them difficult to hit in combat. Any attempt to attack or shoot a creature with this trait is subject to drag. Stealth does not affect use of Glimcraft.

tough.name: Tough
tough.description: Tough creatures are harder to damage, having developed a natural resilience. Divide any damage done to a tough creature in half, rounding down. 1 damage has no effect, 2 damage does 1 damage, and so on. This applies to all damage resulting from close combat attacks, ranged attacks, and Glimcraft.
